evidence.skill_rule: A skill pagecraft-openclaw exige que falhas ACP criticas nao tenham fallback manual silencioso; devem ser registadas como bloqueio.
evidence.impeccable_reference: A referencia impeccable-pagecraft foi revista; a run existente inclui design-brief, design-spec, ticket de reparacao de typography/hierarchy e evidencia de impeccable detect com pass.

existing_evaluation.local_artifact_result: The generated page is reported as usable and passes local JSON, static HTML, browser, proofreader, markdown and impeccable checks.

browser_check.title: Fracoes equivalentes na parcela Minecraft
browser_check.h1: Que parte da parcela fica plantada?

existing_evaluation.decision: A pagina passa como artefacto local experimental, mas a run da skill nao passa como end-to-end ACP autonomo.

run_manifest.conclusion: Local artefact generation and browser verification passed, but the OpenClaw ACP end-to-end run is not yet autonomous because a non-interactive permission prompt can stop the evaluator/manifest closure.

pedagogical_and_design_alignment.docspec: Includes 2.º ano age band, 30 minute session, fractions objectives, MEM alignment, Minecraft maker connection, observable assessment and three differentiation levels.
pedagogical_and_design_alignment.proofreader: Pass true with low severity and no issues.
pedagogical_and_design_alignment.design: Design brief and spec follow PageCraft/Impeccable integration: product register, self-contained UI, accessible controls, responsive layout, no remote assets or modal-first pattern.

evidence.git_status_note: Existing slug artifacts are untracked in the worktree; outputs/lessons/_last_architect_prompt.md was already modified and was not touched for this closure.

blockers.description: The OpenClaw ACP end-to-end run did not close autonomously because a command path required an interactive permission prompt in a non-interactive context.
blockers.impact: The existing page can be accepted only as a local experimental artifact; the pagecraft-openclaw skill run cannot be marked as a passing autonomous ACP run.

blockers.description: The evaluator and manifest closure were completed by the parent/orchestrator after ACP failure, not by a fully autonomous ACP evaluator phase.
blockers.impact: This violates the intended ACP specialization flow for a clean end-to-end validation.

recommendation: Keep the existing page and artifacts as a useful experimental PageCraft output, but do not mark pagecraft-openclaw as ACP-validated yet. Fix the ACP runtime permission policy or constrain verifier commands so the evaluator can run non-interactively, then repeat only the ACP closure/evaluator path before publishing.
